content:
On a fresh checkout, running make rust-analyzer is not fully functional (e.g. broken proc macros, missing binding files). It currently requires either running a full build first or running prepare target before rust-analyzer. This is quite bad development experience because the issue only becomes apparent later when the LSP fails on a proc macro or binding types/functions.

I think we should either update the documentation to recommend make prepare rust-analyzer, or add prepare as a dependency of the rust-analyzer target by default. What do you think?
Oh, not enough people subscribed to this channel I see, perhaps I should move this to the General channel? cc @Miguel Ojeda

I think it is fine; if we never start using new channels then we never will I guess (perhaps we could make it a default channel for new people in case that helps). @Tamir Duberstein and @Jesung Yang will see it, so I wouldn't worry.

Doc changes are usually fine (as long as we don't overdo it so that the docs are too long that nobody reads them), so I think that is a simple/good way to do it.

Regarding adding a dependency on prepare, it is not really required to generate the file itself, so I am not convinced... (And, while I guess it is unlikely, someone out there may be already relying on that property?)

Another alternative could be to print a stderr informational message about it on the rust-analyzer target. That way no one misses it (I hope nobody is relying on that target being completely silent...)
